description: Role-specific instruction preamble generation: lt-gov receives instructions to orchestrate workers (dgov pane create/wait/review/close) rather than edit code directly, plus environment variable DGOV_SKIP_GOVERNOR_CHECK
Worktree assignment override: lt-gov panes without existing_worktree default to running on main project_root directly (no worktree) because they orchestrate rather than edit
Branch name logic: lt-gov panes use empty branch_name since they run on main, avoiding worktree-attached branch conflicts
Environment setup for lt-gov: adds DGOV_SKIP_GOVERNOR_CHECK=1 and DGOV_PROJECT_ROOT to env vars
Merge guard: rejects merging lt-gov panes because they don't produce code - only orchestrate workers - and return error saying nothing to merge
Dashboard pane grouping: lt-gov panes are grouped separately from worker panes so their children appear indented beneath them in the UI hierarchy
Dashboard LT-GOV visual styling: renders lt-gov panes with diamond bullet (◊) and bold magenta color to distinguish from worker panes
